For positive integers $x_1,\ldots, x_n$ define $G(x_1, \ldots, x_n)$ to be the sum of their $\frac{n(n-1)}{2}$ pairwise greatest common divisors. Notice that $G(x_1, \ldots, x_n) \ge \frac{n(n-1)}{2}$ since the greatest common divisor of any two positive integers is at least 1. Note that by a well-known identity, the greatest common divisor of any pair $x_i, x_j$ relates to their least common multiple via $\gcd(x_i, x_j) \cdot \text{lcm}(x_i, x_j) = x_i x_j$. We say that an integer $n \geq 2$ is \emph{artificial} if there exist $n$ different positive integers $a_1, ..., a_n$ such that 
\[a_1 + \cdots + a_n = G(a_1, \ldots, a_n) +1.\]
Find the sum of all artificial integers $m$ in the range $2 \leq m \leq 40$.
